events.event_description: First U.S. mission established to the Federation of the Centre of America, with John Williams presenting credentials as Chargé d'Affaires.

evidence.quote: John Williams, Chargé d'Affaires. Appointed: 1825-12-29. Credentials presented: 1826-05-03. Made farewell calls 1826-12-01. Note: Commissioned to the Federation of the Centre of America.

events.event_description: John Williams made farewell calls, ending the first U.S. mission to the Federation of the Centre of America. No successor was appointed until 1833.

evidence.quote: John Williams, Chargé d'Affaires. Appointed: 1825-12-29. Credentials presented: 1826-05-03. Made farewell calls 1826-12-01. Note: Commissioned to the Federation of the Centre of America.

events.event_description: U.S. mission re-established to the Republic of Central America, with Witt Charles Gerrit De presenting credentials as Chargé d'Affaires.

evidence.quote: Witt Charles Gerrit De, Chargé d'Affaires. Appointed: 1833-01-29. Credentials presented: 1833-12-17. Left post about 1839-01-01. Note: Commissioned to the Republic of Central of America.

events.event_description: Witt Charles Gerrit De left post, ending the U.S. mission to the Republic of Central America. No successor was appointed until 1848.

evidence.quote: Witt Charles Gerrit De, Chargé d'Affaires. Appointed: 1833-01-29. Credentials presented: 1833-12-17. Left post about 1839-01-01. Note: Commissioned to the Republic of Central of America.

events.event_description: U.S. mission re-established to the Republic of Guatemala, with Elijah Hise presenting credentials as Chargé d'Affaires.

evidence.quote: Elijah Hise, Chargé d'Affaires. Appointed: 1848-03-31. Credentials presented: 1849-01-31. Presented recall about 1849-06-23. Note: Commissioned to the Republic of Guatemala.

events.event_description: Normal diplomatic relations interrupted when the new government of Guatemala was unrecognized by the United States; McMillin left post on January 5, 1922.

evidence.quote: Benton Mcmillin, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1919-09-23. Credentials presented: 1920-01-15. Normal relations interrupted on 1921-12-06. Note: The new government of Guatemala was still unrecognized by the United States when McMillin left post on January 5, 1922.

events.event_description: U.S. Legation re-established in Guatemala with Arthur H Geissler presenting credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, restoring normal diplomatic relations.

evidence.quote: Arthur H Geissler, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1922-05-24. Credentials presented: 1922-07-31. Left post on 1930-01-23.

events.event_description: U.S. Legation elevated to Embassy with Boaz Walton Long presenting credentials as the first Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary to Guatemala.

evidence.quote: Boaz Walton Long, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1943-03-27. Credentials presented: 1943-05-19. Left post on 1945-04-11.
